mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0 within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with elevated housing starts signal a synchronized expansion in household balance sheets and physical investment, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and pushes S&P 500 forward returns higher over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight monetary policy, an increase in industrial production indicates resilient corporate capacity utilization and pricing power, which supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative in over 50% of observations where INDPRO rises while FEDFUNDS is above 0.5%, invalidating the supply-side resilience premium.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 2.5 percent confirms an acceleration in aggregate demand, triggering upward earnings revisions across cyclical sectors and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters where real GDP growth surpasses 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in unemployment and stable consumer price inflation signal a healthy non-inflationary labor market recovery, which reduces policy uncertainty and compresses equity risk premiums, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of rolling 3-year windows, or the cross-product term in a predictive regression yields a negative and significant coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which directly boosts corporate top-line growth expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward information coefficient remains below 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window, or monthly INDPRO increases consistently precede negative 5-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.50 percent while consumer prices rise above 2.0 percent annually, real interest rates turn deeply negative, forcing investors into risk assets and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months where FEDFUNDS stays below 0.50%, invalidating the liquidity-driven risk-on premise.
